response.justification: The candidate is a Staff-level engineer with nearly two decades of experience, currently at Stripe, applying for an entry-level position. His technical skills comfortably exceed every requirement — TypeScript, Node.js, React, REST APIs, Kubernetes, even LLM/MCP tooling — but the seniority mismatch is severe. An entry-level salary band and growth trajectory almost certainly cannot retain him, making this a poor mutual fit despite strong raw capability. The resume also contains a confusing entry ('Founder, Datadog' describing an unrelated open-source geo-data project) that would need clarification, and his German is basic for a likely German-market, high on-site role.
strengths: Deep TypeScript/Node.js and React experience across major companies (Stripe, Shopify)
Hands-on Kubernetes, Docker, AWS, and LLM/MCP integration experience matching every nice-to-have
Strong engineering culture background: TDD, CI/CD, testing, and knowledge-sharing via workshops
concerns: Extreme overqualification for an entry-level role — high flight and compensation-mismatch risk
Resume inconsistency: 'Founder, Datadog' entry appears mislabeled and undermines document accuracy
Basic German and unstated motivation for downleveling in a high on-site, likely German-speaking environment

key_factors.factor: Technical stack alignment (TypeScript, React, Node, K8s, LLM)
